{\n275\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n276\t\n277\t    // Create: root (0) -> level2 (2) -> level6 (6)\n278\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n279\t test_exit_to_scope_rapid_successive_exits() {\n296\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n297\t\n298\t    // Create deep nesting\n299\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"a\".to_string()));\n300\t    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"b\".to_string()));\n301\t    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"c\".to_string()));\n302\t    stack.enter_scope(8, 4, Some(\"d\".to_string()));\n303\t\n304\t    // Rapid successive exits without intermediate operations\n305\t    stack.exit_to_scope(6);  // 8 -> 6\n306\t    stack.exit_to_scope(2);  // 6 -> 2\n307\t    stack.exit_to_scope(0);  // 2 -> 0\n308\t\n309\t Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n\n    // Exit all the way to root\n stack.exit_to_scope(5);\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(0).is_some()); // Root still exists\n\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(0).is_some()); // Root still {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_f36002681f3248f699653693","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n69\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\n70\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n71\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\n72\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n73\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\n74\t\n75\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n76\t///\n77\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n78\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n79\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n80\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n81\tpub struct Scope {\n82\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n83\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n84\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n85\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n86\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n87\t    pub start_line: usize,\n88\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n89\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n90\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n91\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n92\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n93\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n94\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n95\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\timpl Scope {\n99\t    /// Create a new scope\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// # Arguments\n102\t    ///\n103\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n104\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n105\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// # Examples\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// ```\n110\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n111\t    ///\n112\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n113\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n114\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n115\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n116\t    /// ```\n117\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n118\t        Self {\n119\t            indent_level,\n120\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n121\t            start_line,\n122\t            parent_key,\n123\t            is_flow_style: false,\n124\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n125\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n126\t        }\n127\t    }\n128\t\n129\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n130\t    ///\n131\t    /// # Arguments\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n134\t    ///\n135\t    /// # Returns\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// # Examples\n140\t    ///\n141\t    /// ```\n142\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n143\t    ///\n144\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n145\t    ///\n146\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n147\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n148\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n149\t    /// ```\n150\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n151\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n155\t    ///\n156\t    /// # Arguments\n157\t    ///\n158\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n159\t    ///\n160\t    /// # Returns\n161\t    ///\n162\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n163\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n164\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n168\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n169\t        self.keys.len()\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n173\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n174\t        self.keys.clear();\n175\t    }\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n179\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n180\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n181\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n182\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n183\t        }\n184\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n189\t///\n190\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n191\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n192\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n193\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n194\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n195\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n196\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n197\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n198\t    base_indent: usize,\n199\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n200\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n201\t    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n202\t    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n203\t    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n204\t    last_indent: usize,\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\timpl ScopeStack {\n208\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n209\t    ///\n210\t    /// # Arguments\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n213\t    ///\n214\t    /// # Examples\n215\t    ///\n216\t    /// ```\n217\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n218\t    ///\n219\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n220\t    /// ```\n221\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n222\t        Self {\n223\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n224\t            base_indent,\n225\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n226\t            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\n227\t            last_indent: 0,\n228\t        }\n229\t    }\n230\t\n231\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n232\t    ///\n233\t    /// # Panics\n234\t    ///\n235\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n236\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n237\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// # Panics\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n245\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n246\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// # Arguments\n252\t    ///\n253\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n254\t    ///\n255\t    /// # Returns\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n258\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n259\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n260\t    }\n261\t\n262\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n263\t    ///\n264\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n265\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n266\t    ///\n267\t    /// # Arguments\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n270\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n271\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n272\t    ///\n273\t    /// # Examples\n274\t    ///\n275\t    /// ```\n276\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n277\t    ///\n278\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n279\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n280\t    /// ```\n281\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n282\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\n283\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n284\t        {\n285\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n286\t            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n287\t            log_debug!(\n288\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n289\t                scope_type,\n290\t                line,\n291\t                indent_level,\n292\t                parent_info,\n293\t                self.depth(),\n294\t                self.get_scope_path()\n295\t            );\n296\t        }\n297\t\n298\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n299\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n300\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n301\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n302\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n303\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n304\t\n305\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n306\t            let before_depth = self.depth();\n307\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n308\t\n309\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n310\t            {\n311\t                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n312\t                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n313\t                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n314\t                    .collect();\n315\t                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n316\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n317\t                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n318\t                             scopes_to_remove);\n319\t                }\n320\t            }\n321\t\n322\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n323\t\n324\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n325\t            {\n326\t                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n327\t                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n328\t                if removed_count > 0 {\n329\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n330\t                             removed_count,\n331\t                             before_path,\n332\t                             self.get_scope_path());\n333\t                }\n334\t            }\n335\t\n336\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n337\t\n338\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n339\t            {\n340\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n341\t            }\n342\t        } else {\n343\t            // Create new scope\n344\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n345\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n346\t\n347\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n348\t            {\n349\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n350\t            }\n351\t        }\n352\t\n353\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n354\t        {\n355\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n356\t        }\n357\t    }\n358\t\n359\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n360\t    ///\n361\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n362\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n363\t    ///\n364\t    /// # Arguments\n365\t    ///\n366\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n367\t    ///\n368\t    /// # Examples\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// ```\n371\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n372\t    ///\n373\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n374\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n375\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n376\t    /// ```\n377\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n378\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n379\t        {\n380\t            log_debug!(\n381\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n382\t                target_indent,\n383\t                self.depth(),\n384\t                self.current_indent(),\n385\t                self.get_scope_path()\n386\t            );\n387\t        }\n388\t\n389\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n390\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n391\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n392\t            {\n393\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n394\t            }\n395\t            return;\n396\t        }\n397\t\n398\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n399\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n400\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n401\t            .count() == 0;\n402\t\n403\t        if would_be_empty {\n404\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n405\t            {\n406\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n407\t            }\n408\t            // Keep at least the root scope\n409\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n410\t            return;\n411\t        }\n412\t\n413\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n414\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n415\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n416\t\n417\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n418\t        {\n419\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n420\t        }\n421\t\n422\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n423\t        // In normal YAML parsing, when exiting to target_indent, there should already be a scope\n424\t        // at that level (created when we entered that scope via enter_scope).\n425\t        // However, in edge cases (e.g., manually creating weird state, certain invalid YAML),\n426\t        // we might not have a scope at the target level.\n427\t        //\n428\t        // We handle this by:\n429\t        // 1. If a scope exists at target_indent, we're done\n430\t        // 2. If no scope at target_indent, we create a fallback scope there\n431\t        //    (this handles edge cases gracefully and maintains correct indent tracking)\n432\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n433\t            // No scope at target level - create a fallback\n434\t            // This is unusual but we handle it gracefully\n435\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n436\t            {\n437\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at target_indent={}, creating fallback scope\",\n438\t                         target_indent);\n439\t            }\n440\t            let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n441\t            self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n442\t        }\n443\t\n444\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n445\t        {\n446\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n447\t        }\n448\t    }\n449\t\n450\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n451\t    ///\n452\t    /// # Arguments\n453\t    ///\n454\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n455\t    ///\n456\t    /// # Returns\n457\t    ///\n458\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n459\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n460\t        self.scopes.last()\n461\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n462\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n463\t    }\n464\t\n465\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// # Arguments\n470\t    ///\n471\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n472\t    ///\n473\t    /// # Returns\n474\t    ///\n475\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n476\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n477\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n478\t    }\n479\t\n480\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n481\t    ///\n482\t    /// # Arguments\n483\t    ///\n484\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n485\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n486\t    ///\n487\t    /// # Returns\n488\t    ///\n489\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n490\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n491\t    ///\n492\t    /// # Examples\n493\t    ///\n494\t    /// ```\n495\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n496\t    ///\n497\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n498\t    ///\n499\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n500\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n501\t    ///\n502\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n503\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n504\t    /// ```\n505\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n506\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n507\t\n508\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n509\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n510\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n511\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n512\t                key: key.to_string(),\n513\t                scope_path,\n514\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n515\t                duplicate_line: line,\n516\t            })\n517\t        } else {\n518\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n519\t            scope.add_key(key);\n520\t            Ok(())\n521\t        }\n522\t    }\n523\t\n524\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n525\t    ///\n526\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n527\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// # Returns\n530\t    ///\n531\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n532\t    ///\n533\t    /// # Examples\n534\t    ///\n535\t    /// ```\n536\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n537\t    ///\n538\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n539\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n540\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n541\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n542\t    /// ```\n543\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n544\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n545\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n546\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n547\t                path.push(key.clone());\n548\t            }\n549\t        }\n550\t        path.join(\".\")\n551\t    }\n552\t\n553\t    /// Get current indent level\n554\t    ///\n555\t    /// # Returns\n556\t    ///\n557\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n558\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n559\t        self.scopes.last()\n560\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n561\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n562\t    }\n563\t\n564\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n565\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n566\t        self.scopes.len()\n567\t    }\n568\t\n569\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n570\t    ///\n571\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n572\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n573\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n574\t        self.clear_indent_transitions();\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n578\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n579\t        self.base_indent\n580\t    }\n581\t\n582\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n583\t    ///\n584\t    /// # Arguments\n585\t    ///\n586\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n587\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n588\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n589\t    }\n590\t\n591\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n592\t    ///\n593\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n594\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n595\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n596\t    ///\n597\t    /// # Arguments\n598\t    ///\n599\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n600\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n601\t    ///\n602\t    /// # Examples\n603\t    ///\n604\t    /// ```\n605\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n606\t    ///\n607\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n608\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n609\t    /// ```\n610\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n611\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n612\t        {\n613\t            log_debug!(\n614\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n615\t                line,\n616\t                indent_level,\n617\t                self.depth(),\n618\t                self.get_scope_path()\n619\t            );\n620\t        }\n621\t\n622\t        // Remove all scopes at or deeper than this level\n623\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we clear any existing scope at this level\n624\t        // (including parent mappings) and all deeper scopes, then add a new sequence scope\n625\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n626\t        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n627\t\n628\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n629\t        {\n630\t            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n631\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level)\n632\t                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n633\t                .collect();\n634\t            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n635\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} scopes at or deeper than indent={}: {:?}\",\n636\t                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n637\t                         indent_level,\n638\t                         scopes_to_remove);\n639\t            }\n640\t        }\n641\t\n642\t        // Only retain scopes shallower than indent_level\n643\t        // Sequence scopes completely replace any mapping at the same level\n644\t        // (unlike sibling mappings which preserve the parent)\n645\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n646\t\n647\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n648\t        {\n649\t            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n650\t            if removed_count > 0 {\n651\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n652\t                         removed_count,\n653\t                         before_path,\n654\t                         self.get_scope_path());\n655\t            }\n656\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes at or deeper than this level (replaces any parent mapping)\", indent_level);\n657\t        }\n658\t\n659\t        // Always create a new scope for each sequence item\n660\t        // Each sequence item needs a fresh scope - we don't reuse existing sequence scopes\n661\t        let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n662\t        new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n663\t        self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n664\t        new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n665\t        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n666\t\n667\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n668\t        {\n669\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n670\t        }\n671\t\n672\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n673\t        {\n674\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n675\t        }\n676\t    }\n677\t\n678\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n679\t    ///\n680\t    /// # Returns\n681\t    ///\n682\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n683\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n684\t        self.scopes.last()\n685\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n686\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n687\t    }\n688\t\n689\t    /// Record an indent transition\n690\t    ///\n691\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n692\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n693\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n694\t    ///\n695\t    /// # Arguments\n696\t    ///\n697\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n698\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n699\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n700\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n701\t    ///\n702\t    /// # Examples\n703\t    ///\n704\t    /// ```\n705\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n706\t    ///\n707\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n708\t    /// stack.record_indent_transition(5, 2, false, \"  # blank line with indent\");\n709\t    /// ```\n710\t    pub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\n711\t        // Only record if the indent actually changed\n712\t        if new_indent != self.last_indent {\n713\t            let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n714\t                line_number,\n715\t                self.last_indent,\n716\t                new_indent,\n717\t                has_key,\n718\t                raw_line,\n719\t            );\n720\t            self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\n721\t            self.last_indent = new_indent;\n722\t\n723\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n724\t            {\n725\t                let direction = if new_indent > self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1) {\n726\t                    \"increase\"\n727\t                } else {\n728\t                    \"decrease\"\n729\t                };\n730\t                let key_status = if has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n731\t                log_debug!(\n732\t                    \"[INDENT TRANSITION] line={}, {}→{}, {}, raw='{}'\",\n733\t                    line_number,\n734\t                    self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1),\n735\t                    new_indent,\n736\t                    key_status,\n737\t                    raw_line.trim()\n738\t                );\n739\t            }\n740\t        }\n741\t    }\n742\t\n743\t    /// Get all recorded indent transitions\n744\t    ///\n745\t    /// # Returns\n746\t    ///\n747\t    /// A slice of all indent transitions recorded during parsing\n748\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self) -> &[IndentTransition] {\n749\t        &self.indent_transitions\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    /// Get indent transitions without keys\n753\t    ///\n754\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines without key tokens,\n755\t    /// such as blank lines or comments.\n756\t    ///\n757\t    /// # Returns\n758\t    ///\n759\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred without keys\n760\t    pub fn get_transitions_without_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n761\t        self.indent_transitions\n762\t            .iter()\n763\t            .filter(|t| t.is_without_key())\n764\t            .collect()\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    /// Get indent transitions with keys\n768\t    ///\n769\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines with key tokens.\n770\t    ///\n771\t    /// # Returns\n772\t    ///\n773\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred with keys\n774\t    pub fn get_transitions_with_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n775\t        self.indent_transitions\n776\t            .iter()\n777\t            .filter(|t| t.has_key)\n778\t            .collect()\n779\t    }\n780\t\n781\t    /// Clear all indent transitions\n782\t    ///\n783\t    /// This is typically called when resetting the scope stack for a new document.\n784\t    pub fn clear_indent_transitions(&mut self) {\n785\t        self.indent_transitions.clear();\n786\t        self.last_indent = 0;\n787\t    }\n788\t\n789\t    /// Get the last recorded indent level\n790\t    ///\n791\t    /// # Returns\n792\t    ///\n793\t    /// The most recent indent level recorded\n794\t    pub fn get_last_indent(&self) -> usize {\n795\t        self.last_indent\n796\t    }\n797\t\n798\t    /// Set the last indent level directly\n799\t    ///\n800\t    /// This can be used to initialize the indent tracking or to correct it after\n801\t    /// document markers or other special constructs.\n802\t    ///\n803\t    /// # Arguments\n804\t    ///\n805\t    /// * `indent` - The indent level to set\n806\t    pub fn set_last_indent(&mut self, indent: usize) {\n807\t        self.last_indent = indent;\n808\t    }\n809\t\n810\t    /// Process an indent transition without a key token\n811\t    ///\n812\t    /// This method handles indentation changes that occur on lines without key tokens,\n813\t    /// such as blank lines or comments. It determines if the indent change represents\n814\t    /// a valid scope transition and triggers the appropriate scope entry/exit.\n815\t    ///\n816\t    /// # Arguments\n817\t    ///\n818\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n819\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n820\t    ///\n821\t    /// # Returns\n822\t    ///\n823\t    /// `true` if a scope transition occurred, `false` otherwise\n824\t    ///\n825\t    /// # Examples\n826\t    ///\n827\t    /// ```\n828\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n829\t transitioned = stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(5, 0);\n834\t    /// assert!(transitioned); // Should exit to root scope\n835\t    /// ```\n836\t    pub fn process_indent_transition_without_key(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize) -> bool {\n837\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n838\t\n839\t        // Compare new indent with current scope indent\n840\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n841\t\n842\t        match new_indent.cmp(&current_indent) {\n843\t            Ordering::Greater => {\n844\t                // Indent increased - but without a key, this is unusual\n845\t                // We don't enter a new scope without a parent key\n846\t                // Just record the transition for tracking purposes\n847\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n848\t                {\n849\t                    log_debug!(\n850\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (increase), NO SCOPE ENTRY (no parent key)\",\n851\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent\n852\t                    );\n853\t                }\n854\t                false\n855\t            }\n856\t            Ordering::Less => {\n857\t                // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n858\t                // This is valid even without a key (e.g., blank line at end of nested block)\n859\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n860\t                {\n861\t                    let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n862\t                    log_debug!(\n863\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (decrease), exiting scope: '{}'\",\n864\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent, before_path\n865\t                    );\n866\t                }\n867\t\n868\t                self.exit_to_scope(new_indent);\n869\t                true\n870\t            }\n871\t            Ordering::Equal => {\n872\t                // Same indent - no scope change needed\n873\t                false\n874\t            }\n875\t        }\n876\t    }\n877\t}\n878\t\n879\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n880\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut self.get_scope_path())\n883\t    }\n884\t}\n885\t\n886\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n887\t///\n888\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n889\t/// {\n979\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n980\t    ///\n981\t    /// # Returns\n982\t    ///\n983\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n984\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1013\t        match self {\n1014\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n1015\t                write!(f,  }\n1025\t}\n1026\t\n1027\t/// Extract key context from a line\n1028\t///\n1029\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n1030\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n1031\t///\n1032\t/// # Arguments\n1033\t///\n1034\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n1035\t///\n1036\t/// # Returns\n1037\t///\n1038\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, Option<KeyContext> {\n1054\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n1055\t\n1056\t    // Find colon position\n1057\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n1058\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n1059\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n1060\t\n1061\t    // Skip if key is empty\n1062\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n1063\t    if key.is_empty() {\n1064\t        return None;\n1065\t    }\n1066\t\n1067\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n1068\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n1069\t        return None;\n1070\t    }\n1071\t\n1072\t    // Strip sequence dash from key if present\n1073\t    // Handles: \"- name: value\" -> key should be \"name\", not \"- name\"\n1074\t    let actual_key = if key.starts_with(\"- \") {\n1075\t        // Remove the \"- \" prefix (dash and space)\n1076\t        key[2..].trim()\n1077\t    } else if key.starts_with('-') && key.len() > 1 {\n1078\t        // Dash followed by non-space (invalid, but handle gracefully)\n1079\t        &key[1..]\n1080\t    } else {\n1081\t        key\n1082\t    };\n1083\t\n1084\t    // Skip if actual key is empty after stripping dash\n1085\t    if actual_key.is_empty() {\n1086\t        return None;\n1087\t    }\n1088\t\n1089\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n1090\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n1091\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: actual_key.to_string() }\n1092\t    } else {\n1093\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n1094\t            key: actual_key.to_string(),\n1095\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n1096\t        }\n1097\t    };\n1098\t\n1099\t    Some(context)\n1100\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t/// Get leading whitespace characters\n1111\t///\n1112\t/// # Examples\n1113\t///\n1114\t/// ```\n1115\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n1116\t///\n1117\t/// occurred (1-indexed)\n1149\t    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n1150\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n1151\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n1152\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content\n1153\t    pub fn new(line_number: usize, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) -> }\n1182\t}\n1183\t\n1184\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransition {\n1185\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1186\t        let direction = if self.is_increase() {\n1187\t
